name: Chicken in Moroccan Style W/Pickled Lemons &
recipeIngredient: 2 Three pound chickens cut into serving pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Rub the chicken pieces well with a mixture of 2 tablespoons salt and 4 finely chopped garlic cloves. Let stand for 1 hour for flavors to penetrate, then wipe off the garlic salt.
2. Mix 1 cup vegetable oil, 2 teaspoons ginger, 1 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon black pepper, and a pinch of saffron. Rub this mixture onto the chicken pieces and add any remaining oil mixture. Marinate covered in the refrigerator for at least 8 hours or overnight.
3. To cook, place the marinated chicken pieces in a large pot with grated onions, remaining chopped garlic, butter, chicken broth, and 2 cups water. Simmer until tender, about 40 to 45 minutes.
4. Remove the chicken and quickly reduce the liquid to a thick, rich sauce by stirring frequently. Add the olives and pickled lemon slices, return the chicken to the sauce, and reheat gently.
5. Serve with couscous or rice pilaf and a bowl of extra pickled lemons.
Pickled Lemons:
1. Cut 6 lemons into thin slices (about 1/4 inch thick), including the peel. Place in a colander and sprinkle heavily with coarse salt, covering with plastic wrap.
2. Drain over a bowl for 24 hours until the lemon pieces are limp and most of the juice is drawn out.
3. Pack the drained lemon slices into a quart jar, sprinkling with about 2 tablespoons additional salt. Pour vegetable oil over the lemons to cover them completely. Seal the jar and let stand at room temperature for 1 to 3 weeks until softened and mellow, not bitter.
